answer:
\boxed{B}  

The study shows that patients with very high CA 19-9 levels (≥900 IU/ml) had similar survival to those with high levels (400–899 IU/ml), and no statistical difference was found between these groups. Additionally, patients whose CA 19-9 normalized after surgery had survival equivalent to the control group. Therefore, very high CA 19-9 levels alone are not a contraindication to pancreaticoduodenectomy.
